page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-56 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration delivers 56 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 28 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 112 GB of system memory, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory subsystem performance is highly competitive, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score in the top 10% and a strong cached read-only memory bandwidth of 3817.65 GB/sec. However, uncached large-block memory read and write speeds fall into the average tier. Cryptographic operations and database tasks perform strongly, as demonstrated by high PassMark encryption scores and 61.68 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

Given the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and the lack of local storage, this instance is optimized for compute-bound workloads rather than memory-intensive or storage-heavy applications. It is well-suited for software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and medium-scale LLM inference, where its strong multi-threaded performance can be fully utilized. The hardware profile offers high compute density, making it a cost-efficient choice for parallel processing tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-56 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It delivers 56 dedicated vCPUs across 28 physical cores, paired with 112 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores in the top 25%, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. It excels in parallel processing tasks, software compilation, and cryptographic operations, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth remains average. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for compute-dense workloads that do not require large memory allocations or local NVMe drives, making it suitable for software development pipelines, ray tracing, and Redis-based caching layers.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring fifty-six dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs optimized for parallel processing and multi-threaded workloads.

Equipped with 112 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Strong multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks
